Name: curvature_aware_mixed_precision_quantization
Title: Curvature-Aware Mixed Precision Quantization: A Training-Free Approach Leveraging Geometric Diagnostics
Short Hypothesis: Incorporating curvature-aware geometric diagnostics into mixed-precision quantization can yield superior efficiency and accuracy by providing a more principled optimization framework, distinct from existing heuristic-based approaches.
Related Work: Current state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods, such as EMQ and InfoQ, rely heavily on heuristic proxies or computationally expensive training-based methods. This proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature-aware geometric diagnostics that directly inform the quantization strategy, fundamentally altering the optimization landscape.
Abstract: In the domain of mixed-precision quantization (MPQ), existing frameworks often resort to heuristic methods or computationally intensive training processes to determine optimal quantization configurations. This paper proposes a novel curvature-aware mixed precision quantization framework that leverages geometric diagnostics to achieve training-free quantization strategies. By employing deterministic spectral estimators and curvature metrics, our approach systematically identifies quantization configurations that maintain model performance while significantly reducing computational overhead. We validate our framework through extensive experiments on several benchmark datasets, demonstrating that our method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art approaches in both accuracy and efficiency. Furthermore, we provide a comprehensive analysis of gradient covariance decay, feature entanglement, and Hessian conditioning, establishing a robust mathematical foundation for our method. The proposed curvature-aware framework not only enhances the interpretability of quantization decisions but also sets a new standard for training-free mixed-precision optimizations in deep learning.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed framework on standard datasets such as ImageNet and CIFAR-10, comparing quantization accuracy and efficiency against existing methods like EMQ and InfoQ.
Conduct ablation studies to assess the impact of curvature-aware diagnostics on quantization decisions and overall model performance.
Utilize metrics such as accuracy drop, computational overhead, and quantization error to quantify performance improvements.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential challenges in adapting geometric diagnostics to various model architectures.
Uncertainty regarding the generalizability of the framework across different types of neural networks.
Dependency on the quality of the geometric diagnostics to provide meaningful insights for quantization.
